title: salix tenuijulis var. alberti
intro: Salix tenuijulis var. alberti, also known as the Albert's willow, is a variety of the narrowleaf willow, a type of deciduous shrub or small tree. The medicinal properties of the Albert's willow lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it a natural anti-inflammatory and pain reliever. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and relieving headaches, fever, and sore throats, as well as aiding in the treatment of arthritis and rheumatism. The herbal preparations of the Albert's willow can be made into a tea, tincture, or salve, and can also be used as a topical application for skin irritations and wounds.
